name: Store Charts in OCI Registries
description: Push, pull, and manage Helm charts using OCI-compatible container registries like GitHub Container Registry, Docker Hub, and ECR.
trendingReason: OCI registry support is now stable in Helm 3 and becoming the preferred distribution method

steps.title: Login to an OCI registry
steps.description: Authenticate with your container registry to push and pull charts.
steps.tip: Use a personal access token with read:packages and write:packages scopes for GitHub Container Registry.

steps.title: Package the chart
steps.description: Create a chart archive ready for pushing to the registry.

steps.title: Push a chart to the OCI registry
steps.description: Upload the packaged chart to your registry.

steps.title: Pull a chart from the OCI registry
steps.description: Download a chart from the registry to your local machine.

steps.title: Install directly from OCI registry
steps.description: Install a chart directly from an OCI reference without pulling first.

steps.title: View chart metadata in the registry
steps.description: Inspect chart details without downloading it.

fullScript:
#!/bin/bash
# Helm OCI Registry Workflow
set -euo pipefail

CHART_DIR=${1:?"Usage: $0 <chart-dir> <registry-url>"}
REGISTRY=${2:?"Missing registry URL (e.g., oci://ghcr.io/my-org/charts)"}

# Extract chart name and version from Chart.yaml
CHART_NAME=$(grep '^name:' "$CHART_DIR/Chart.yaml" | awk '{print $2}')
CHART_VERSION=$(grep '^version:' "$CHART_DIR/Chart.yaml" | awk '{print $2}')

echo "=== Chart: $CHART_NAME v$CHART_VERSION ==="

echo ""
echo "=== Linting ==="
helm lint "$CHART_DIR" --strict

echo ""
echo "=== Packaging ==="
helm package "$CHART_DIR"

PACKAGE_FILE="$CHART_NAME-$CHART_VERSION.tgz"

echo ""
echo "=== Pushing to $REGISTRY ==="
helm push "$PACKAGE_FILE" "$REGISTRY"

echo ""
echo "=== Verifying ==="
helm show chart "$REGISTRY/$CHART_NAME" --version "$CHART_VERSION"

echo ""
echo "Chart published: $REGISTRY/$CHART_NAME:$CHART_VERSION"
echo "Install with: helm install <release> $REGISTRY/$CHART_NAME --version $CHART_VERSION"

faq.question: What is the advantage of OCI registries over traditional Helm repos?
faq.answer: OCI registries reuse existing container registry infrastructure, provide immutable chart references, support signature verification, and eliminate the need to maintain a separate chart repository server with an index.yaml file.

faq.question: How do I use AWS ECR as a Helm chart registry?
faq.answer: Login with 'aws ecr get-login-password | helm registry login --username AWS --password-stdin <account>.dkr.ecr.<region>.amazonaws.com'. Then push with 'helm push chart.tgz oci://<account>.dkr.ecr.<region>.amazonaws.com'.

faq.question: Do I still need helm repo add for OCI registries?
faq.answer: No. OCI charts are referenced directly by their full URL (oci://registry/path/chart). The 'helm repo add' command is only for traditional HTTP-based chart repositories.
